messages.content:
Explain why side-channel attacks are a concern in cryptographic implementations.

The AES instructions on the ARMv8 platform do not have an algorithm that is 'intrinsically resistant' to side-channel attacks. NOTE: the vendor reportedly offers the position 'while power side channel attacks ... are possible, they are not directly caused by or related to the Arm architecture.'

Side-channel attacks are concerning in cryptographic implementations because they exploit physical leakages (e.g., power consumption) to extract sensitive information, bypassing traditional security controls without directly compromising cryptographic algorithms.
